perturbation.original_text: 2. “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature. The existence and subject matter of the parties’ discussions shall be treated as Confidential Information. Confidential Information may also include information disclosed to Owner by third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature. The existence and subject matter of the parties’ discussions shall NOT be treated as Confidential Information. Confidential Information may also include information disclosed to Owner by third parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding whether the existence and subject matter of discussions are treated as confidential. Section 2 initially defines it as Confidential Information, but the modified text explicitly states it is NOT. This creates uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature. The existence and subject matter of the parties’ discussions shall NOT be treated as Confidential Information. Confidential Information may also include information disclosed to Owner by third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 2 to explicitly state that 'the existence and subject matter of the parties’ discussions shall NOT be treated as Confidential Information'. The contradicted text in the same section has the same change. This creates an in-text contradiction because the definition now excludes something that was initially included. The contradiction is direct and would cause confusion regarding the scope of confidentiality, as both sections contradict each other on whether discussions are confidential, which falls under the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Return of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner and shall be promptly returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request or upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Return of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner and shall be returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request; however, upon termination of this Agreement, the Recipient may retain such materials indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the return of materials upon termination. Initially, all materials must be returned. The modified text allows the Recipient to retain the materials indefinitely upon termination, conflicting with the initial obligation to return them, creating uncertainty about the post-termination obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner and shall be returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request; however, upon termination of this Agreement, the Recipient may retain such materials indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 8 to allow the Recipient to retain confidential materials indefinitely upon termination, which directly contradicts the original requirement of returning all materials upon termination, as initially stated in both the original text and implicitly in the contradicted text, before the change was introduced. This inconsistency regarding the return obligation creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires five (5) years from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”). Either party may terminate this Agreement earlier by giving thirty (30) days prior written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires five (5) years from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”). This Agreement cannot be terminated earlier and must run for the full three year term.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding early termination. The original text allows termination with 30 days notice, while the modified text states the agreement cannot be terminated early and must run for the full three-year term. This introduces uncertainty regarding the ability to terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires five (5) years from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”). This Agreement cannot be terminated earlier and must run for the full three year term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 10, removing the clause allowing early termination with 30 days' notice and replacing it with a statement that the agreement cannot be terminated early and must run for the full three-year term. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text. This creates a direct contradiction, as the original clause and its removal are not in alignment, thus there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and not for any third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and also for any third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to all employees and agents of Recipient, regardless of whether they are required to have the information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original non-use and non-disclosure obligations. Originally, information could only be used for the authorized purpose and not for third-party benefit, and disclosure was limited. Now, the modified text allows use for third-party benefit and unlimited disclosure, creating significant in-text contradiction and negating the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and also for any third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to all employees and agents of Recipient, regardless of whether they are required to have the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 directly contradicts the original intent of non-use and non-disclosure. The original text restricted the use of confidential information to the authorized purpose and disallowed its use for the benefit of third parties, while also limiting disclosure to employees and agents who needed the information. The changed text now allows the use of confidential information for the benefit of third parties and permits unlimited disclosure to all employees and agents, regardless of need. This creates a clear and direct conflict with the original intent and the contradicted text, effectively negating the purpose of the NDA.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No Obligation. Nothing herein shall obligate either party to proceed with any transaction between them, and each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate the discussions contemplated by this Agreement concerning the business opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Obligation to Transact. Each party is obligated to proceed with a transaction between them, and neither party has the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate the discussions contemplated by this Agreement concerning the business opportunity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction to the 'No Obligation' clause. The original clause explicitly states that neither party is obligated to proceed with a transaction. However, the changed clause states that each party *is* obligated to proceed and cannot terminate discussions, which directly in text contradicts the freedom to terminate or not engage in a transaction. This creates great uncertianty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligation to Transact. Each party is obligated to proceed with a transaction between them, and neither party has the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate the discussions contemplated by this Agreement concerning the business opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6, 'Obligation to Transact,' directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text, which requires parties to proceed with a transaction and eliminates their discretion to terminate discussions. This is a direct contradiction. The explanation clarifies that the changed clause mandates a transaction and prohibits termination, conflicting with the freedom previously established. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction,' where different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the other party for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, the harmed party may be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement will not cause irreparable harm to the other party and monetary damages are adequate. Accordingly, the harmed party is not entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original 'Remedies' section allows for injunctive relief, acknowledging potential irreparable harm. The change explicitly states that no irreparable harm will occur and monetary damages are adequate, thereby removing the right to injunctive or equitable relief. This creates a direct in-text contradiction and limits the remedies available, introducing uncertainty about enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement will not cause irreparable harm to the other party and monetary damages are adequate. Accordingly, the harmed party is not entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 explicitly states that a breach of the agreement will not cause irreparable harm and monetary damages are adequate, thus removing the possibility of injunctive or equitable relief. The contradicted text states the exact same thing, which is a contradiction to the original text. By removing the ability to seek injunctive relief in the changed text, this creates a direct conflict and inconsistency with the original intent, based on the perturbation explanation, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.
